description: Building without a .dockerignore so the build context ships .git, .env, and node_modules into the image or build cache.
body: Failure mode: with no .dockerignore, COPY pulls the entire working tree — including .git history, .env files, local credentials, and bulky local dependencies — into the build context and potentially the image, leaking secrets and bloating the build. Detection signals: build context is huge/slow; .env or .git appears in the image; secret scan flags copied credential files. Do not COPY the whole directory without an ignore file. Safer fallback: add a .dockerignore excluding VCS metadata, env/secret files, local deps, and build output; copy only what the image needs and rotate any secret that was included. Risk boundary: escalate and rotate if a secret entered the context rather than assuming the image is private.
